title: Sorbus pluripinnata
taxonomy: Sorbus pluripinnata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Sorbus, which consists of various species of rowans. The family Rosaceae encompasses a wide range of flowering plants, including roses, apples, and pears. This classification places Sorbus pluripinnata within the order Rosales, along with other related plants. As a member of the Rosaceae family, Sorbus pluripinnata shares characteristics with other flowering plants in this group.
uses: Sorbus pluripinnata is a species of rowan tree native to the Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions. Its bark and roots are used to make a decoction, which is believed to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is also used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand the extent of its medicinal uses and potential side effects.
benefits: Sorbus pluripinnata, a lesser-known species of rowan tree, is believed to have various health benefits due to its rich content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The leaves and bark of the tree are traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The plant is also rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Research on Sorbus pluripinnata is limited, but its potential health benefits make it an area of interest for further study. Further investigation is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Sorbus pluripinnata for medicinal purposes.
properties: Sorbus pluripinnata, also known as the many-lobed rowan, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. Research suggests that extracts from the plant may possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. These properties could be beneficial in the treatment of various health conditions, including infections and diseases related to oxidative stress. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Sorbus pluripinnata. Further research is required to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
constituents: Sorbus pluripinnata, also known as the Chinese rowan, contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These compounds have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which may contribute to their medicinal properties. Sorbus pluripinnata's roots and bark contain higher concentrations of phenolic acids, including ferulic and sinapic acids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Sorbus pluripinnata's active constituents.
parts: Sorbus pluripinnata, a species of mountain ash, has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The bark, leaves, and fruits of the plant are commonly used to treat various health conditions. The bark is known to have anti-inflammatory properties and is often used to treat fever and sore throats. The leaves and fruits are used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. preparations: Sorbus pluripinnata is not a well-documented plant in herbal medicine, but there is limited information on its traditional uses in some parts of the world.
